scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every documented Clockodo endpoint is enumerated below, covered by a stream/write or explicitly excluded with a specific reason.

excluded.reason: single-customer detail already reachable via the customers stream's listing; no separate stream needed
single-project detail already reachable via the projects stream's listing; no separate stream needed
single-service detail already reachable via the services stream's listing; no separate stream needed
single-user detail already reachable via the users stream's listing; no separate stream needed
invites/provisions a new co-worker seat, a billing-seat-consuming admin action distinct from ordinary record creation
updates a co-worker's role/access-control settings (e.g. can_add_customers, boss); an admin-scoped action, no demonstrated write demand for ordinary sync
irreversibly disables a co-worker's access to the account, a billing-seat/access-revocation admin action
single-team detail already reachable via the teams stream's listing; no separate stream needed
single-surcharge-model detail already reachable via the surcharges stream's listing; no separate stream needed
creates a new surcharge model; no demonstrated write demand alongside the read-only surcharges stream (narrow payroll-configuration object)
updates a surcharge model; no demonstrated write demand
deletes a surcharge model; no demonstrated write demand
single-lump-sum-service detail already reachable via the lumpsum_services stream's listing; no separate stream needed
single-entry detail already reachable via the entries stream's listing; no separate stream needed

creates a time entry; the polymorphic entry body (plain time entry / lump-sum value / lump-sum-service variants, discriminated by 'type') has no single dialect-expressible record_schema shape yet, deferred pending a decided per-type write strategy
updates a time entry; deferred alongside the polymorphic create-body decision above
deletes a time entry; deferred alongside the polymorphic create/update decision above so create/update/delete land together in a future increment
queries free-text descriptions used across time/lump-sum entries; a narrow autocomplete-style lookup resource, no demonstrated read demand
requires a mandatory (time_since, time_until, grouping) triple with no natural default; grouped/aggregated entry view layered over the entries stream already covered
bulk-edits every time entry matching a filter across a date range in one call
bulk-deletes every time entry matching a filter across a date range in one irreversible call
single-carryover-row detail already reachable via the holidays_carry stream's listing; no separate stream needed
creates a holiday-carryover row; sensitive HR/payroll-adjacent mutation, no demonstrated write demand alongside the read-only holidays_carry stream
updates a holiday-carryover row; sensitive HR/payroll-adjacent mutation, no demonstrated write demand
deletes a holiday-carryover row; sensitive HR/payroll-adjacent mutation, no demonstrated write demand
single-quota-row detail already reachable via the holidays_quota stream's listing; no separate stream needed
creates a holiday-quota row; sensitive HR/payroll-adjacent mutation, no demonstrated write demand alongside the read-only holidays_quota stream
updates a holiday-quota row; sensitive HR/payroll-adjacent mutation, no demonstrated write demand
deletes a holiday-quota row; sensitive HR/payroll-adjacent mutation, no demonstrated write demand
single-carryover-row detail already reachable via the overtime_carry stream's listing; no separate stream needed
creates an overtime-carryover row; sensitive HR/payroll-adjacent mutation, no demonstrated write demand alongside the read-only overtime_carry stream
updates an overtime-carryover row; sensitive HR/payroll-adjacent mutation, no demonstrated write demand
deletes an overtime-carryover row; sensitive HR/payroll-adjacent mutation, no demonstrated write demand
single-target-hours-row detail already reachable via the target_hours stream's listing; no separate stream needed
creates a target-hours (planned-hours) configuration row; HR/payroll-adjacent configuration mutation, no demonstrated write demand alongside the read-only target_hours stream
updates a target-hours configuration row; no demonstrated write demand
deletes a target-hours configuration row; no demonstrated write demand
manages per-user holiday-group-period assignment history; narrow HR-configuration join resource, no demonstrated read/write demand
single-absence detail already reachable via the absences stream's listing; no separate stream needed
creates an absence (vacation/sick-leave) request; sensitive HR-workflow mutation, no demonstrated write demand alongside the read-only absences stream
updates/approves an absence request; sensitive HR-workflow mutation, no demonstrated write demand
deletes an absence request; sensitive HR-workflow mutation, no demonstrated write demand
controls the live stopwatch (start/stop a running timer via GET-as-action, per Clockodo's own docs); an imperative device-control action, not a data listing
per-user customer/project access-rights lookup; narrow authorization-introspection resource, no demonstrated read demand
per-user service access-rights lookup; narrow authorization-introspection resource, no demonstrated read demand
generates an aggregated co-worker report document, not a listing of underlying records
creates a brand-new Clockodo account (an org-level admin/billing action, not per-record data), and API-key-scoped connections are conventionally already bound to one account
queries computed work-time totals derived from entries already covered by the entries stream; an aggregated-view resource, no demonstrated additional read demand
queries work-time change requests; HR-workflow object, no demonstrated read demand
submits a work-time change request; HR-workflow action, not ordinary record data sync
approves/rejects a work-time change request; HR-workflow action
webhook subscription management; push-delivery configuration, not a pollable data resource this connector's pull-sync model reads
